rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARIA CHUN TAO PAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/1/22. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] The petition caption does not disclose that fees and reimbursement of costs are being requested. [CRC 7.102.] No courtesy copy of the proof of service or Notice of Proposed Action referenced in the petition and filed contemporaneously with the petition. Notice was not given to the DHCS, which filed a Request for Special Notice on 2/2/16 and has not withdrawn it. Notice of Proposed Action is a procedure for a personal representative with IAEA authority to take certain actions during estate administration and WITHOUT COURT SUPERVISION. [Probate Code Sect. 10580(a).] A petition for final distribution is the vehicle for reporting on the estate administration and REQUIRES COURT SUPERVISION (approval). Ordinarily, waivers of accounting signed by all interested parties are required to be filed when a petition for final distribution is based on waiver of accounting. An appearance will likely be required, after the proposed order is received by the assigned Examiner, for the Court to determine if the mere absence of objections to the Notice of Proposed Action was sufficient to be considered a waiver of accounting by all heirs and sufficient disclosure of all relief sought but not disclosed in the petition caption. It would have been helpful if the petition had disclosed when the payouts mentioned in paragraph 9 of the petition were received, as the related Civil case was dismissed in March 2018. (The notice to the FTB indicated that Letters were issued on 2/24/2017; they were issued on 2/24/2016.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/19/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees

It appears only $6,634.23 in city and county of SF property taxes were paid during this account period. d. $6,441.00 in state/fed taxes. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/24/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DONALD MAJOR SMEDLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (728 Le Conte Ave, San Francisco, Ca 94124)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/26/22. It appears that the real property might be community property, as the administrator is the surviving spouse. At the outset of these proceedings, she filed a Declaration of Real Property, claiming that 100% interest in the real property would pass to her (although the decedent is also survived by more than one issue). The case file does not reflect her written election to have her one-half interest in the estate subject to estate administration. [Probate Code Section 13502 and LRSF 14.35 I.5.] A supplement to the amended report of sale must include a copy of the vesting deed, showing how title to the real property is held.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF TAYLOR SKIDMORE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The former court-appointed counsel for the conservatee requests modification the Court's previous ex parte order for her fees and costs in the total amount of $756.66 payable from the county. The former court-appointed counsel renewed her request for payment of fees and costs in the total amount of $2,010 from the conservatorship estate and agreed to reimburse the county for the $756.66 payment she has received. The former court-appointed counsel requests the modification relief as a motion for reconsideration under CCP section 1008(b). 2) The conservator requests distribution of the remaining conservatorship asset to the State Treasurer pursuant to sections 11900(a) & 11902(a)(1) , as there are no known beneficiaries of the estate otherwise entitled to distribution. Section 2467(a) provides that the conservator continues to have the duty of custody and conservation of the estate after the death of the conservatee pending the delivery thereof to the "personal representative" of the conservatee's estate or other disposition according to law. The "personal representative" is defined in section 58(a) to specifically include a public administrator acting pursuant to Section 7660. If the total value of the estate does not exceed $50,000, a public administrator has authority to take possession of the estate and act as personal representative for summary administration without court authorization under section 7660(a)(2). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCES L. CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/15/2022. 1) The 12/16/2021 Supplemental Declaration amends material terms of the petition and must be filed as an amendment and noticed. (See CRC 7.3) 2) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. Ending balances shown in the account must be reconciled to the statements or letters, which must be filed. [See LRSF 14.34 D. prior note indicated 14.35 D. which was incorrect LRSF #] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/24/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BENJAMIN M. PICETTI 2007 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For The Settlement And Approval Of The First Annual Trustees Account Of Management Of The Assets Of The Trust For The First Year May 21 2019 Through May 30 2020 Request For Orders
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/15/22. The Court's review of Mr. Melbostad and his law firm's billing records indicate that the lion's share of the time billed was for services in his capacity as "Trustee" for "trust administration services" and not for "legal services provided for trust administration" (see letter attached as Exhibit G to Supplemental Declaration filed on 8/18/21). What standard should the Court use to distinguish time spent on legal services for trust administration, compensaable at $525/hour under the contract with the temporary conservator, from services Mr. Melbostad provided in his capacity as Trustee, which are not compensable under the agreement with the temporary conservator? A verified declaration addressing this issue must be filed, and a filed stamped copy delivered, at least two weeks prior to the continuance date. Any responses must be filed, and a filed stamped copy delivered, at least 1 week prior.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DANIEL GOMEZ URIONAGUENA & MANUELA MUNOZ DE GOMEZ REV. LIVING TRUST, U/T/A DTD 6/29/94 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Title To Certain Assets
rulings.Rulings: continued to 2/22/22. when a petition is filed without its exhibits, an amended petition must be filed. it is not sufficient to file an "errata" and ask the court to consider the attachement petition the operative pleading. a second amended petition must be filed and noticed for 30 days prior to the new hearing date. in addition, a person "nominated" as executor is not the same as a person appointed to represent the estate. Letters mppointing a PR for Daniel's estate must be filed prior to the hearing. mailed notice is not sufficient for WFT - see PC 851(a). a file stamped copy of the second amended petiiton and proposed order must be delivered to the examiner in room 202 (emailed or faxed copies are not accepted). new deadline for curing all issues is noon on 2/10/22.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF A. NATIVIDAD PARAS NISCE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE BARBARA J BARDARO REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions: Appiontment Of Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/01/2022 (no earlier date available). 1) The Court's record indicates that the second amended petition was accepted and processed by the clerk on 12/23/2021, even though the filing was backdated as of 12/20/2021. Notice of the second amended petition had to be given on or after 12/23/2021, but according to the the proof of service on file, notice was given on 12/20/2021. New 30-day notice of the continued hearing must be given. 2) Notice must also be given to Timothy Wanek, a person having a joint legal custody of and residing with the contingent minor beneficiary. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPH RICHARD PIERCE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate is set for 1/31/2023. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 1/31/2023. Appearance will be required on 1/31/2023 if no petition has been filed.

Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. Court to determine how to proceed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TIMOTHY YEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although "proposed Administrator" is NOT a RELATIONSHIP to the decedent (he is presumably the decedent's brother), and the attorney does not need to verify a pleading required to be verified by a [proposed] fiduciary. A petitioner does not need to give notice to himself. [Probate Code Sect. 1201.] Status of closing the estate is set for 1/31/2023. A petition for final distribution or for additional time for estate administration must be filed by no later than 1/18/2023. Appearance required on 1/31/2023 if not filed.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/8/22 due to last-minute emailed objections. Any objections must be filed in time to deliver courtesy copies to the assigned Examiner by 1/28/22.

Note: the Notices of Proposed Action have not been reviewed as no personal representative has been appointed. Objections to the petition must be made on pleading paper and filing fees must be paid.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIEN HEDQUIST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition was not answered. If the petitioner's verified supplement is filed before this hearing and indicates that the decedent was not survived by any issue of a predeceased child, the Court might wish to admit the will. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: IFEANYI AGUOJI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF BIRTH
rulings.Rulings: continued to 2/8/22. a petition to establish fact of birth may be brought in the county of residence of the person seeking the order, or the county in which the birth is alleged to have occurred. this birth was alleged to be in Nigeria, and the petitioner states his residence is in Brooklyn, New York. Under these facts, this petition is not properly presented to the San Francisco court. Petitioner must file support for this court to hear this petition.
